perturbation.original_text: 4. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party and, upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return to the disclosing Party all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, or any part or reproduction thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party, except any improvements, modifications, or derivations made by the receiving Party, which shall become the sole property of the receiving Party. Upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return all original CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, excluding any improvements, modifications, or derivations thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original text asserts that all CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION remains the property of the disclosing party. The modified text contradicts this by stating that any improvements, modifications, or derivations made by the receiving party become the receiving party's property. This creates a contradiction because the overall agreement is about protecting the discloser's CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Now the receiving party can claim ownership of improvements, derivations, or modifications, potentially undermining the original intent of confidentiality and ownership rights for the disclosing party. This directly contradict the original meaning in paragraph 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION will remain the property of the disclosing Party, except any improvements, modifications, or derivations made by the receiving Party, which shall become the sole property of the receiving Party. Upon request of the disclosing Party, the receiving Party shall promptly return all original CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, excluding any improvements, modifications, or derivations thereof.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that ALL CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION remains the property of the disclosing party. The changed text creates a contradiction by stating that improvements, modifications, or derivations made by the receiving party become the receiving party's property. This directly contradicts the initial statement regarding ownership of ALL confidential information. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which confirms this contradiction. The explanation specifies that the receiving party can now claim ownership of improvements, derivations, or modifications, undermining the confidentiality and ownership rights of the disclosing party. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 5. The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will expire five (5) years from the termination of the JRA.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will expire five (5) years from the termination of the JRA, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states the confidentiality obligations expire five years after the termination of the JRA. The added phrase 'unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties' introduces an exception. It is now unclear whether the five-year expiration is fixed or can be extended indefinitely. This undermines the certainty of the original expiration clause by creating a contradiction. This affect section 5 as a whole as the reader doesn't know if the period ends after 5 years or not
perturbation.contradicted_text: The obligations of each and every Party, and each employee and officer of each Party Under this Agreement will expire five (5) years from the termination of the JRA, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states a fixed expiration of obligations five years after the JRA termination. The changed text adds 'unless otherwise agreed to in writing by both parties', introducing an exception that allows for the extension of these obligations indefinitely. This directly contradicts the original fixed expiration, creating uncertainty and a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which is simply a copy of the changed text. The addition undermines the certainty of the original expiration clause.

perturbation.original_text: 7. The terms and provisions of this Agreement will inure to the benefit of the Parties, their respective successors and assigns and will be binding on said successors and assigns. This paragraph notwithstanding, neither Party may disclose any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any successor or assign absent prior written consent of the disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. The terms and provisions of this Agreement will inure to the benefit of the Parties, their respective successors and assigns and will be binding on said successors and assigns. Notwithstanding the first sentence of this paragraph, either Party may disclose any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any successor or assign at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that while the agreement binds successors and assigns, CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION cannot be disclosed to them without the disclosing party's written consent. The changed text contradicts this by stating that either party can disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to successors/assigns at their discretion, completely removing the requirement of prior consent. This change creates in-text contradiction within the section, undermining the confidential protections for the discloser.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms and provisions of this Agreement will inure to the benefit of the Parties, their respective successors and assigns and will be binding on said successors and assigns. Notwithstanding the first sentence of this paragraph, either Party may disclose any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to any successor or assign at their discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original intention of Section 7. The original text disallowed disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION to successors or assigns without prior written consent, creating a restriction. The changed text removes this restriction by stating that either party can disclose such information at their discretion. The contradiction text is identical to the changed text, and therefore exhibits this contradiction. The original text and the modified text introduce directly contradictory obligations in the same clause, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by Structural Flaws.

perturbation.original_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that no right or license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is granted to any other Party or any other person by this Agreement or by any disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. The Parties understand and agree that a limited, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for purposes directly related to the JRA is granted to each Party. No other right or license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is granted to any other Party or any other person by this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that no rights or licenses are granted under any patent, patent application, or know-how through the agreement or the disclosure of CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. The added sentence contradicts this by granting a limited, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. This contradiction introduces uncertainty about the scope of rights conferred by the agreement and affects the reader's understanding of whether any implied rights have been granted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties understand and agree that a limited, non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION for purposes directly related to the JRA is granted to each Party. No other right or license under any patent, patent application, or know-how is granted to any other Party or any other person by this Agreement or by any disclosure of any CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text states 'no right or license... is granted'. The changed text then says 'a limited, non-exclusive, royalty-free license... is granted'. This creates a direct conflict, as a license is explicitly granted where none was before. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', which includes the original language stating that no such license is granted. This creates a clear and direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT The Parties acknowledge that each owns certain CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, as defined herein, which might relate to the inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA; and The Parties are willing to disclose to each other such necessary CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided each Party preserves the confidential nature of the other Party's INFORMATION and uses it solely for purposes of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT The Parties acknowledge that each owns certain CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, as defined herein, which might relate to the inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA; and The Parties are willing to disclose to each other such necessary CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided each Party preserves the confidential nature of the other Party's INFORMATION; the Parties are under no obligations for the confidential nature of the INFORMATION.
perturbation.explanation: In the opening paragraph, the original text emphasizes that parties are willing to disclose CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided that each party preserves the confidential nature of the other's information. The added clause contradicts this by stating that the parties are under no obligations for the confidential nature of the information. This creates a fundamental in-text contradiction because it negates the core purpose of a non-disclosure agreement by implying that confidentiality is not required, making the entire agreement self-defeating.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT The Parties acknowledge that each owns certain CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION, as defined herein, which might relate to the inventions, conceptions, ideas, know-how, discoveries, processes, machines, manufactures, compositions of matter, formulations, processes, biological material, biological methods, or any improvements thereof, whether or not patentable or suitable for other form of exclusive right or legal protection, conceived, made or derived during the course of Work within the Project under this JRA; and The Parties are willing to disclose to each other such necessary CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided each Party preserves the confidential nature of the other Party's INFORMATION; the Parties are under no obligations for the confidential nature of the INFORMATION.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the phrase 'the Parties are under no obligations for the confidential nature of the INFORMATION' to the original text of the non-disclosure agreement. This directly contradicts the earlier statement that 'The Parties are willing to disclose to each other such necessary CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION provided each Party preserves the confidential nature of the other Party's INFORMATION'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it nullifies the very purpose of a non-disclosure agreement, implying that confidentiality is not required, thus making the agreement self-defeating. Since the Contradicted Text is in the same location, this confirms the in-text contradiction exists.
